input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

samples.output: <think>A man in a blue shirt and white cap kneels on a boat facing a calm lake. White text overlay reads, "You are not gonna believe what is swimming towards our boat." Upper right graphic resolves to show a dodo bird logo. Man shifts gaze left while holding the boat edge, watching ripples on the water near trees.</think><answer></answer>

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-4] A man in a blue shirt and white cap kneels on a boat facing a calm lake. Text reads "You are not gonna believe what is swimming towards our boat." A logo resolves top right. Ripples disturb the water near trees. [4-6] The caption vanishes, replaced by "a KITTEN" with a yellow arrow pointing to a circled area where a small animal head breaks the surface. The man smiles. [6-10] New text appears: "These guys were out in their boat" with orange highlighting. The man watches the small animal swimming steadily toward the boat. [10-14] Text updates to "when they saw someone tiny swimming toward them" with orange highlighting. The man observes the light-colored animal paddling closer to the vessel's edge.

[16-20] A man in a blue shirt and beige shorts leans over a maroon boat gunwale. Yellow text "This ain't real" displays. He extends his arm toward the water. Text updates to "so they pulled him up," highlighting "pulled him up" in orange.
[20-24] The man lifts a wet, light-brown kitten by the scruff from the water. Yellow text "Never in my life ..." appears. He places the kitten on the gray boat deck, where it stands on all fours near his legs.
[24-26] The man crouches on the deck, extending his hand to gently pet the back of the wet orange kitten near yellow fishing rods.
[26-30] The man stops petting and looks toward the lake. White text "Then they realized he wasn't alone" appears, highlighting "he wasn't alone" in orange. Finally, yellow text "Oh there's more, Jason!" displays as the kitten stands near the rods.

[30-34] A man in a blue shirt and 'YETI' hat crouches on a boat deck near yellow fishing rods while a wet orange kitten walks across the gray surface. Yellow text reads 'I just seen a kitten jump on the ... on the ground.' [34-36] The man looks toward the camera as the subtitle changes to 'I just saw it jump in the water.' [36-38] The man stands up from his crouching position while the kitten remains standing near the rods. [38-42] He crouches back down as new text appears: 'Oh god, I bet somebody put them out.' The kitten walks closer to his gray sneakers. [42-44] The man bends forward, reaching his hand down toward the kitten, while the text updates to 'Never in my life, have I seen anything like this.'

recent_thinks: [14-16] The bottom text overlay vanishes. The man in the blue shirt leans further over the boat's edge, extending his right arm down toward the water. The small swimming animal reaches the side of the vessel, positioning itself directly next to the man's outstretched hand near the hull.

[44-46] The man in the blue shirt remains bent over on the boat deck near the wet orange kitten. The yellow subtitle text changes to "Look, it's coming out of the weeds right now." The kitten stands near the man's sneakers facing the water, with a tree-lined shore visible across the lake.

[46-48] The yellow subtitle text vanishes. The man in the blue shirt and cap looks out toward the tree-lined shore across the calm water. The wet orange kitten is no longer clearly visible in the foreground. The camera angle tilts upward, revealing more of the green trees and their reflection on the lake surface.

[48-50] The man in the blue shirt stands looking toward the tree-lined shore. A circular magnified overlay appears on the water near the bank, revealing a small white object floating in the weeds. Simultaneously, white text reading "Oh my god." appears at the bottom of the screen.

queries.question: What text overlay appears with a yellow arrow pointing to the animal in the water? A. This ain't real. B. a KITTEN C. so they pulled him up D. These guys were out in their boat

samples.output: <think>A circular magnified overlay continues to highlight a small white object floating in the water near the grassy bank. The white text caption reading "Oh my god." disappears from the bottom center of the screen. The man in the blue shirt remains partially visible on the left edge, facing the shore.</think><answer></answer>

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
